driveSettings.googleApiKey: You are redesigning the HOME PAGE / MAIN MENU for my mobile portrait card game called “Jester’s Grid.”  Do NOT rebuild the whole game. Do NOT change the actual gameplay rules. Do NOT break any existing routes, game screens, saved data, or logic. This task is ONLY for the main menu / homepage UI and for setting up the UI system so I can replace interface pieces later with my own custom PNG artwork.  ================================================== GAME STYLE ==================================================  The game is called:  JESTER’S GRID  The art direction should be:  - 2D hand-drawn fantasy card game UI - dark mode parchment / ink / storybook style - mature but playful jester theme - red, blue, cream, gold, and dark navy palette - slightly sketchy line art - soft rendered shading - simple readable forms - not hyper-detailed - not glossy modern sci-fi - not overly realistic - whimsical but strategic - feels like a serious brainy card game with mischievous personality  The style should feel like: - hand-drawn jester storybook - fantasy card game - paper texture - ink outlines - custom illustrated buttons - red and blue jester motifs - gold bells, stars, diamonds, cards, masks, ribbons  Use the attached/reference menu design as inspiration: - large title area - boy and girl jesters around the title - profile/progression panel - large Play / Decks / Ranked cards - bottom row of circular icon buttons - dark textured background - parchment cards and gold trim  ================================================== MAIN MENU LAYOUT ==================================================  The screen is mobile portrait.  Structure the homepage vertically like this:  1. Top corner buttons - Top-left: News button - Top-right: Rewards button - These should be circular parchment/gold buttons with simple hand-drawn icons.  2. Hero title section - Large centered title: JESTER’S GRID - Use a parchment banner, dark navy/red backing, or hand-drawn title plaque. - Add small jester hat / bell / star decorations. - Under the title, add the tagline:  “Where every step is a joke, and every joke changes fate.”  3. Character mascots - Add space for two jester mascots:   - Girl jester on the left   - Boy jester on the right - They should frame the logo. - For now, use placeholder illustrated panels or CSS placeholders. - IMPORTANT: The code must be prepared so I can later replace these with my own custom PNGs. - The mascots should be positioned responsively so they do not cover buttons or text.  4. Player profile / progression panel - A wide horizontal panel under the hero area. - Shows:   - player avatar   - player name: Jester   - rank: Trickster   - level badge: 14   - XP bar, for example 350 / 1000   - coin count, for example 1,250   - gem count, for example 340   - small plus buttons beside currency - Style this as a dark parchment/ink panel with gold border.  5. Main action cards - Three large rectangular menu cards:   - PLAY   - DECKS   - RANKED - Each card should look like a parchment playing card. - Each should have a simple icon area:   - Play: jester card icon   - Decks: two card backs   - Ranked: crown / jester hat / laurel - These should be large, readable, and very tappable. - Add hover/press animations:   - slight lift   - subtle scale   - shadow   - small gold glow - On mobile, pressing should feel tactile.  6. Bottom icon menu - A row of smaller circular buttons:   - Shop   - Collection   - Quests   - Settings   - Profile   - Exit - Each button should have a simple hand-drawn icon placeholder. - Keep them evenly spaced and readable. - They should not take too much vertical space.  ================================================== CUSTOM PNG UI SYSTEM REQUIREMENT ==================================================  This is extremely important.  Set up the homepage code so that later I can replace CSS-made UI pieces with custom PNG artwork I draw.  Build a clean UI asset system.  Add a central object or config section in JavaScript like:  const UI_ASSETS = {   logoBanner: "",   titleBacking: "",   girlJester: "",   boyJester: "",   profileFrame: "",   playCard: "",   decksCard: "",   rankedCard: "",   bottomButtonFrame: "",   newsIcon: "",   rewardsIcon: "",   coinIcon: "",   gemIcon: "",   cardBackRed: "",   cardBackBlue: "" };  The values can be empty strings for now.  Then create helper behavior: - If an asset path exists, use the PNG as a background image or img element. - If the asset path is empty, fall back to the CSS-drawn version. - This way the homepage works immediately, but can later be upgraded with custom PNGs.  Example behavior: - If UI_ASSETS.girlJester has a PNG path, show that image. - If it is empty, show a placeholder silhouette/card. - If UI_ASSETS.playCard has a PNG path, use it as the button/card background. - If empty, use the styled CSS parchment card.  Do NOT hardcode everything in a way that makes custom PNG replacement difficult.  Use reusable components/classes: - .asset-slot - .png-backed - .fallback-frame - .menu-card - .icon-button - .mascot-slot  Each major UI part should be easy to swap with a PNG later.  ================================================== IMAGE / PNG SLOT DETAILS ==================================================  Create image slots for:  - logo / title banner - girl jester mascot - boy jester mascot - player avatar - profile frame - Play card background - Decks card background - Ranked card background - bottom circular button frame - currency icons - card icons - small decorative corner ornaments  For every image slot: - preserve aspect ratio - use object-fit: contain where needed - never stretch character art weirdly - keep transparent PNGs supported - make sure pointer events still work if an image is used - make sure text remains readable over PNG backgrounds  Use CSS variables for sizing so I can tweak later.  ================================================== RESPONSIVE MOBILE PORTRAIT RULES ==================================================  This must work well on phone screens.  Target: - 390x844 - 393x852 - 412x915 - 430x932  Rules: - No horizontal scrolling. - No text clipping. - No buttons offscreen. - Layout should use safe-area padding for phones. - Main menu should fit in one portrait screen if possible. - If the screen is short, allow gentle vertical scrolling, but avoid messy overflow. - Keep Play / Decks / Ranked cards large. - Bottom icon row should stay compact. - The title must remain readable.  Use CSS with: - clamp() - svh/dvh units where useful - CSS variables - responsive grid/flex - max-width around 480px for the app shell - centered app frame on desktop  ================================================== ANIMATION / GAME FEEL ==================================================  Add subtle homepage polish:  - Title has a very subtle idle float or shimmer. - Jester mascots have subtle idle breathing/bobbing. - Buttons lift on hover and compress on press. - Menu cards have a small “paper card” bounce when tapped. - Currency plus buttons pulse gently. - Bottom icons have small hover/tap bounce. - Rewards button can have a tiny sparkle. - Do not overanimate. Keep it tasteful.  Add CSS animation classes: - .idle-float - .soft-breathe - .tap-bounce - .sparkle - .card-hover  ================================================== INTERACTION PLACEHOLDERS ==================================================  Wire the buttons to placeholder handlers without breaking anything.  For now: - Play button should call handleMenuAction("play") - Decks button should call handleMenuAction("decks") - Ranked button should call handleMenuAction("ranked") - Shop button should call handleMenuAction("shop") - Collection button should call handleMenuAction("collection") - Quests button should call handleMenuAction("quests") - Settings button should call handleMenuAction("settings") - Profile button should call handleMenuAction("profile") - Exit button should call handleMenuAction("exit") - News button should call handleMenuAction("news") - Rewards button should call handleMenuAction("rewards")  Inside handleMenuAction, for now just console.log the action and optionally show a small toast.  Do not implement full navigation unless the project already has routing. If routing exists, connect to existing route functions carefully without breaking anything.  ================================================== VISUAL DETAILS ==================================================  Background: - dark navy paper texture feeling - subtle vignette - faint stars / jester diamonds / sketch marks - not too noisy  Title: - large hand-drawn serif/fantasy lettering feel - gold/cream text - slight ink outline - parchment banner or dark plaque  Panels: - rough hand-drawn border - gold trim - parchment fill or dark navy fill - sketchy imperfections  Buttons: - large readable type - parchment card style - gold outline - icon illustration space - pressable tactile feeling  Colors: Use this approximate palette:  --ink: #10131A --navy: #0B1728 --deep-blue: #173A5E --red: #A33A2E --cream: #F2DFB4 --paper: #E8D3A4 --gold: #C99A3E --soft-gold: #F1C86A --shadow: rgba(0,0,0,0.35)  ================================================== CODE QUALITY ==================================================  Keep the code clean.  Use: - semantic HTML - organized CSS sections - reusable classes - a single config object for UI assets - helper functions for applying image assets - no duplicated event listeners - no broken IDs - no inline spaghetti code  Add comments in the code explaining where I should put PNG asset paths later.  Example:  // Add your custom PNG paths here. // Leave empty to use CSS fallback artwork.  ================================================== DELIVERABLE ==================================================  Generate the full homepage code.  If this is inside my existing app: - integrate it as the main menu/home screen - preserve existing app logic - do not delete gameplay code - do not break the game screen  If this is a standalone prototype: - provide one complete HTML file with CSS and JS included - it should run immediately in the browser  Final result should look like a polished dark-mode Jester’s Grid mobile homepage with: - title - mascots - player profile panel - Play / Decks / Ranked buttons - bottom icon menu - custom PNG-ready UI asset slots - responsive mobile portrait layout
